papers.title: Bonding-favourable facets reduce trap density in wide-bandgap perovskite films
papers.abstract: Preferential exposure of (100)-oriented surfaces lowers deep-level trap formation and strengthens ligand-based passivation, supporting high-voltage wide-bandgap devices and efficient all-perovskite tandems.

papers.title: Halide homogenization suppresses non-radiative recombination in 2-eV bromide-rich perovskites
papers.abstract: Wide-bandgap perovskites are essential for all-perovskite multijunction solar cells but suffer large open-circuit voltage deficits. Here we show that diammonium-halide additives homogenize the halide distribution, reducing interfacial non-radiative recombination and enabling a 1.44 V open-circuit voltage at 86% of the detailed-balance limit. The improved sub-cell yields a 25.1% triple-junction device.

papers.title: Operando ion migration imaging in halide perovskite solar cells
papers.journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society
papers.abstract: We resolve ion migration pathways under operational bias and correlate halide segregation with local phase changes.

papers.title: A perovskite photodetector array for X-ray detection
papers.abstract: A scintillator-free direct X-ray detector based on a thick perovskite single crystal achieves a low detection limit suitable for medical imaging.

papers.title: Trustworthy benchmarks for halide perovskite transistors
papers.abstract: Nature Reviews Materials, Published online: 10 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41578-026-00935-1 Halide perovskite transistors are advancing rapidly, yet reported mobility values remain highly sensitive to material properties, device physics and measurement protocols. Establishing reliable, reproducible and application-specific mobility metrics is essential for translating laboratory performance into credible device technologies.

papers.title: In situ nanocrystal confinement for efficient blue perovskite LEDs
papers.abstract: Nature, Published online: 10 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10596-3 Efficient blue perovskite light-emitting diodes with an external quantum efficiency of 21.8% are achieved through in situ polymerization-driven nanocrystal confinement.

papers.title: Perovskite-driven photoelectrochemical water splitting at 17% solar-to-hydrogen
papers.abstract: An all-perovskite tandem powers an electrochemical flow cell for water splitting, retaining over 60% of peak performance after 180 hours.

papers.title: Crystallinity-guided hybrid conversion for efficient ultrawide-bandgap perovskites in triple-junction solar cells
papers.abstract: Nature Photonics, Published online: 09 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41566-026-01922-2 Tuning the bromine/iodine ratio in evaporated inorganic framework enables the fabrication of hybrid-deposited ultrawide-bandgap perovskites and their use in perovskite/perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells with a certified power conversion efficiency of 27.53%.

papers.title: Author Correction: Stable tin–lead perovskite inks for efficient all-perovskite tandems
papers.abstract: Nature Energy, Published online: 09 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02097-4 Author Correction: Stable tin–lead perovskite inks for efficient all-perovskite tandems

papers.title: Energy & environmental routes to stable narrow-bandgap perovskites
papers.abstract: We review compositional strategies that stabilize narrow-bandgap perovskite absorbers for the bottom sub-cell of all-perovskite tandems.

papers.title: Indoor photovoltaics based on lead-free perovskites
papers.abstract: A lead-free perovskite absorber tuned for indoor PV achieves high efficiency under low-light LED illumination.

papers.title: Ultralow-voltage electrochemical organic light-emitting transistors with pinned and wide lateral recombination
papers.abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 08 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02613-7 Organic light-emitting transistors require a large operating voltage and exhibit a narrow recombination zone, owing to inefficient charge injection. By incorporating an ion transport enhancer into light-emitting polymers, organic light-emitting transistors achieving <3.5-V voltage operation and a maximum recombination zone width of ~267 µm are realized.

papers.title: A self-assembled monolayer via rapid and scalable soak coating for perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: Nature Synthesis, Published online: 04 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44160-026-01089-2 The use of self-assembled monolayers (SAMs) as interfacial hole-selective layers is a key technological enabler for perovskite solar cells (PSCs), but scalable fabrication remains challenging. Here a 5-min soak-coating protocol is developed through SAM molecular design and hydrogen-bond-mediated solvent engineering. High-density monolayers are produced, enabling scalable production of high-efficiency, large-area PSCs.
